creator: mi@kot.ne.mediaone.net
text:
Don Croyle once stated:
 
 => 	The present version of the port is for AStyle-1.6.0. Author's
 => 	latest is 1.8.2 as of March 18, 1999.
 =
 =Yeah, I'm running behind.  For one thing, I don't have a machine
 =running -current at the moment.
 
 I don't either. Truthfully, I think, ports should stress out -stable
 compatability in the first place, since that is, what non-developers
 run (or should run).
  
 => 	My version also removes the dependency on the EGCS compiler,
 => 	which, I believe, makes the port a lot easier to install.
 
 =1.6.0 flat would not compile with gcc 2.7.*.  Now that -current has
 =egcs as the system compiler, the egcs port doesn't need to be
 =installed to build it *on -current*.  The compiler for 3.* hasn't
 =changed, and isn't likely to anytime soon.
 
 No-no :) I did not simply remove the dependency because the compiler
 has changed. My version of port builds on 3.1-stable with the stock
 compiler, and should build just as easily on -current and on the
 2.2.8-stable branch.
 
 => 	The uuencoded tar-ball of the new version of the port is here:
 =
 =Unencoded diffs please.  I'm not even going to bother uudecoding
 =these.
 
 Sorry you don't like those, but my PR contained the entire port's tar-ball --
 the changed/added files are:
 
 	Makefile
 	files/Makefile
 	files/md5
 	patches/patch-aa
 	patches/patch-ab
 
 According to the Handbook's instructions, the ports should be submitted within
 the PR in tar.gz.uu form...
 
 	-mi
